rag_rules: Repair validator diagnostics by adding or modifying AGREE annex clauses in the correct scope
Use only visible identifiers from the current component or its dependencies
Ensure all AGREE expressions have valid types matching their declarations
Place assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes, and bindings in implementation annexes when valid
generic_rules: Minimize repairs to only address the validator diagnostic
Avoid introducing new architecture elements unless required by the diagnostic
Use AGREE scalar types int, real, and bool for declarations
Ensure all AGREE expressions have valid types matching their declarations
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annexes are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Use `eq` for expressions and `const` for constants
Boolean expressions should be of type bool
Integer expressions should be of type int
anti_patterns: Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers not visible in the model
Do not delete clauses unless they are invalid or unresolved
Do not change unrelated architecture elements
irrelevant_topics: AGREE assumptions and guarantees not related to the current diagnostic
Unrelated AADL component types or implementations
